scenario: A team's Web Modeler project has 15 resources (BPMN, DMN, Forms). They want to share the whole project with a new partner organisation's modelers -- readonly is acceptable.
question: Which Web Modeler feature fits?

options.text: Exporting all resources to a ZIP file and emailing it to the partner enables offline work but breaks collaborative real-time co-development. The partner works on a disconnected copy; merging changes back requires manual comparison and conflict resolution. Each round of collaboration requires another export-email-import cycle. Project Sharing via Web Modeler eliminates this workflow by providing live shared access to the same project with proper role-based access control.

Web Modeler Project Sharing enables collaboration by inviting users to a project with one of four roles: Project Admin (full control including sharing), Editor (create and modify resources), Commenter (view and comment only), and Viewer (read-only access). Partner agencies can be invited with the Editor role to create and modify BPMN, DMN, and Form resources in real time. This provides controlled collaborative access without exporting files, without sharing login credentials, and without creating public links.

Creating a public link to the project would make all project resources accessible to anyone with the link, without authentication or role-based access control. This is a significant security exposure for proprietary business process models, DMN decisions, and form schemas. Web Modeler Project Sharing uses invitation-based access with specific roles per collaborator -- targeted, authenticated, and auditable. Public links bypass all access controls and are not a supported Web Modeler feature.

Web Modeler fully supports project sharing and collaborative access with external users and partner agencies. Project Sharing with configurable roles (Viewer, Commenter, Editor, Project Admin) is a documented Web Modeler feature in Camunda 8 SaaS. Claiming this feature is absent would be factually incorrect and would mislead teams into manual file-based collaboration workflows when the platform provides built-in collaborative access management natively. ###

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler has project-level sharing with role-based permissions -- 4 roles per collaboration.md:27-32: Project Admin (edit project + invite), Editor (edit folders/diagrams), Commenter (view + leave comments), Viewer (view only). Invite by email; assign Viewer for read-only access (or Commenter if partner needs to leave feedback).

b.text: Correct. ZIP export loses real-time updates.

c.text: Incorrect. Public links are not the canonical share model.
